papers.title: Localizing and Editing Knowledge in Large Audio-Language Models
papers.authors: Sung Kyun Chung, Jiaheng Dong, Qiuchi Hu
papers.abstract_snippet: Large Audio-Language Models (LALMs) have shown strong performance in speech understanding, making speech a natural interface for accessing factual information. papers.authors: Laya Iyer, Angelina Wang, Sanmi Koyejo
papers.abstract_snippet: Advances in large language models (LLMs) have enabled significant capabilities in audio processing, resulting in state-of-the-art models now known as Large Audio Language Models (LALMs). papers.title: Persuasion Tokens for Editing Factual Knowledge in LLMs
papers.authors: Paul Youssef, Christin Seifert, Jörg Schlötterer
papers.abstract_snippet: In-context knowledge editing (IKE) is a promising technique for updating Large Language Models (LLMs) with new information. papers.title: Retention analysis of edited knowledge after fine-tuning

papers.abstract_snippet: Large language models (LLMs) rely on internal knowledge to solve many downstream tasks, making it crucial to keep them up to date. papers.title: Toward Ultra-Long-Horizon Sequential Model Editing
papers.authors: Mingda Liu, Zhenghan Zhu, Ze'an Miao
papers.abstract_snippet: Model editing has emerged as a practical approach for mitigating factual errors and outdated knowledge in large language models (LLMs). papers.title: REACT: Representation Extraction And Controllable Tuning to Overcome Overfitting in LLM Knowledge Editing
papers.authors: Haitian Zhong, Yuhuan Liu, Ziyang Xu
papers.abstract_snippet: Large language model editing methods frequently suffer from overfitting, wherein factual updates can propagate beyond their intended scope, overemphasizing the edited target even when it's contextually inappropriate. papers.title: How Auditory Knowledge in LLM Backbones Shapes Audio Language Models: A Holistic Evaluation
papers.authors: Ke-Han Lu, Szu-Wei Fu, Chao-Han Huck Yang
papers.abstract_snippet: Large language models (LLMs) have been widely used as knowledge backbones of Large Audio Language Models (LALMs), yet how much auditory knowledge they encode through text-only pre-training and how this affects downstream performance remains unclear. papers.authors: Yifei Wang, Yuheng Chen, Wanting Wen
papers.abstract_snippet: In this paper, we investigate whether Large Language Models (LLMs) actively recall or retrieve their internal repositories of factual knowledge when faced with reasoning tasks.
